title: I can't stop watching this prankster drop cranberry sauce in public and wait
author: Popkin
summary: I can't stop watching videos from Jellytoi, a mischievous prankster who stirs up chaos with a simple can of jellied cranberry sauce. The premise of all Jellytoi's videos goes as follows: a can of cranberry sauce is emptied out in a public space and left in a perfect can shape. —
full_text:
I can't stop watching videos from Jellytoi, a mischievous prankster who stirs up chaos with a simple can of jellied cranberry sauce. The premise of all Jellytoi's videos goes as follows: a can of cranberry sauce is emptied out in a public space and left in a perfect can shape.

Next, Jellytoi sets up a camera and steps back to watch what happens. You'd be surprised at the things that a simple can of cranberry sauce can lead people to do. People stare at it, then approach it cautiously, and often kick it a little to see what happens. Children try to grab it while parents yell, then stare at the sauce themselves for a while.

The pointlessness of these videos is what makes them so addicting to watch. There's something hypnotic about watching strangers encounter an obstacle with no context — the moment of confusion, the hesitant reach, the embarrassed laugh when they realize they've spent two minutes staring at cranberry sauce on a sidewalk. People could easily walk past or around the red pile of jelly, yet something about it draws them in despite their disgust. I find this video series to be far more interesting than reality TV.
